note: partial: T3 entries (LT 1.1, 1.2, 3.2, 5.2, 7.2, 8.3) and LT 1.3 dual-pathway entries not yet added — to be completed in next session using Opus.

dag_validation.status: NOT_VALIDATED — run DAG validation after completing T3 and LT 1.3 entries

criteria.criterion_statement: I can notice physical sensations in my body, identify and name my feelings, and use a calming strategy to help me return to learning activities.

competency_level_descriptors.no_evidence: Self-regulation behaviours not yet observed.
competency_level_descriptors.emerging: Sometimes notices body signals or names feelings with adult prompting.
competency_level_descriptors.developing: Occasionally identifies feelings and attempts calming strategies independently.
competency_level_descriptors.competent: Consistently notices body signals, names feelings, and uses calming strategies to return to learning.
competency_level_descriptors.extending: Consistently self-regulates and can explain their process to others.

criteria.decomposition_rationale: This produces one criterion as the three components (noticing body signals, naming feelings, using strategies) form an integrated self-regulation process observable as a single dispositional behaviour at this developmental level.

criteria.criterion_statement: I can identify a common personal trigger and choose a practised strategy to manage my response.

competency_level_descriptors.no_evidence: Does not identify personal triggers or select strategies.
competency_level_descriptors.emerging: Identifies triggers or selects strategies with adult prompting and support.
competency_level_descriptors.developing: Sometimes identifies triggers and selects appropriate strategies independently.
competency_level_descriptors.competent: Consistently identifies common personal triggers and selects practised strategies to manage responses.
competency_level_descriptors.extending: Consistently demonstrates trigger awareness and strategy selection, explaining choices to others.

criteria.decomposition_rationale: This is a single dispositional behaviour combining trigger awareness with strategic response selection, observable as one integrated competency at this developmental level.

criteria.criterion_statement: I can apply a chosen strategy in a challenging situation and reflect on how well it worked.

competency_level_descriptors.no_evidence: Strategy use and reflection not yet observed.
competency_level_descriptors.emerging: Applies strategies with prompting and reflects briefly when guided.
competency_level_descriptors.developing: Sometimes applies strategies independently and offers simple reflections on effectiveness.
competency_level_descriptors.competent: Consistently applies chosen strategies in challenging situations and reflects thoughtfully on their effectiveness.
competency_level_descriptors.extending: Consistently demonstrates strategic responses and articulates detailed reflections about strategy effectiveness.

criteria.decomposition_rationale: This represents one integrated dispositional behaviour combining strategy application with reflective evaluation, observable as a single competency pattern.

criteria.criterion_statement: I can apply my emotional regulation strategies effectively under stress across different settings and reflect on their effectiveness to inform future improvements.

competency_level_descriptors.no_evidence: Strategy use under stress not yet observed.
competency_level_descriptors.emerging: Applies strategies under stress occasionally with adult prompting and support.
competency_level_descriptors.developing: Applies strategies under stress sometimes independently across familiar settings.
competency_level_descriptors.competent: Consistently applies strategies under stress across different settings and reflects on effectiveness to plan improvements.
competency_level_descriptors.extending: Consistently demonstrates strategic regulation under stress and coaches others in strategy selection.

criteria.decomposition_rationale: This is a single dispositional criterion as it represents one integrated capability - the ability to maintain strategic emotional regulation under stress while engaging in metacognitive reflection across contexts.

criteria.criterion_statement: I can identify emotions in others and respond with appropriate kindness or helpful actions.

competency_level_descriptors.no_evidence: Does not recognize others' emotions or respond caringly.
competency_level_descriptors.emerging: Occasionally names feelings or offers help with adult prompting and guidance.
competency_level_descriptors.developing: Sometimes identifies emotions and responds kindly without consistent prompting from adults.
competency_level_descriptors.competent: Consistently recognizes others' feelings and spontaneously offers kind words or specific helpful actions appropriately.
competency_level_descriptors.extending: Consistently responds empathetically and can explain why their caring response was helpful.

criteria.decomposition_rationale: This is a single dispositional criterion as it captures one integrated empathetic behavior combining emotional recognition with caring response action.

criteria.criterion_statement: Students can describe a situation from another person's perspective and ask if they would like help before acting.

competency_level_descriptors.no_evidence: Does not show perspective-taking or asking before helping.
competency_level_descriptors.emerging: Occasionally describes others' perspectives or asks before helping when prompted.
competency_level_descriptors.developing: Sometimes describes others' perspectives and asks before helping without prompting.
competency_level_descriptors.competent: Consistently describes situations from others' perspectives and asks if they would like help before acting.
competency_level_descriptors.extending: Consistently demonstrates perspective-taking and consent-seeking, explaining why this approach helps others feel respected.

criteria.decomposition_rationale: This is a single dispositional behaviour combining perspective-taking with respectful helping practices, observable as one integrated social skill.

criteria.criterion_statement: I can notice what others say and do in a group, check in when I'm unsure, and adjust my actions to match their stated needs or boundaries.

competency_level_descriptors.no_evidence: Does not observe others' verbal or nonverbal cues.
competency_level_descriptors.emerging: Occasionally notices others' cues with adult prompting and sometimes checks in.
competency_level_descriptors.developing: Sometimes notices others' cues independently and asks clarifying questions when uncertain.
competency_level_descriptors.competent: Consistently observes others' verbal and nonverbal signals, seeks clarification when needed, and adjusts behavior accordingly.
competency_level_descriptors.extending: Models attentive group awareness and can explain how to read social cues.

criteria.decomposition_rationale: This is a single dispositional criterion as it represents one integrated social behavior of awareness and responsive adjustment within group contexts.

criteria.criterion_statement: The student demonstrates relationship repair after conflict through active listening, taking responsibility, and collaborative problem-solving.

competency_level_descriptors.no_evidence: No observed attempts at relationship repair after conflict.
competency_level_descriptors.emerging: Occasionally attempts one aspect of repair when prompted by adults.
competency_level_descriptors.developing: Sometimes initiates repair attempts, demonstrating listening or accountability but not full process.
competency_level_descriptors.competent: Consistently engages in complete repair process including listening, owning responsibility, and reaching mutual agreements.
competency_level_descriptors.extending: Consistently repairs relationships and can explain or model the repair process for others.

criteria.decomposition_rationale: This is a single dispositional behavior requiring integrated demonstration of listening, accountability, and collaborative problem-solving as one coherent relationship repair process.

criteria.criterion_statement: The learner can use their senses to direct attention to one task for a short time.

competency_level_descriptors.no_evidence: Does not sustain attention to task
competency_level_descriptors.emerging: Briefly attends to task with prompting to use senses
competency_level_descriptors.developing: Sometimes uses senses to attend to task for short periods
competency_level_descriptors.competent: Consistently uses senses to direct attention to one task for short time
competency_level_descriptors.extending: Uses senses purposefully and can explain which senses help focus

criteria.decomposition_rationale: This addresses the attentional capacity component of the learning target.

criteria.criterion_statement: The learner can describe how they brought their attention back when it wandered.

competency_level_descriptors.no_evidence: Cannot describe attention recovery
competency_level_descriptors.emerging: Notices attention wandered with adult prompting
competency_level_descriptors.developing: Sometimes describes what helped bring attention back
competency_level_descriptors.competent: Clearly describes specific ways they brought attention back when it wandered
competency_level_descriptors.extending: Describes attention strategies and teaches them to others

criteria.decomposition_rationale: This addresses the metacognitive awareness component which involves different cognitive processes than sustaining attention itself.

criteria.criterion_statement: I can apply a practised attention strategy while engaged in a task.

competency_level_descriptors.no_evidence: Does not use attention strategies during tasks.
competency_level_descriptors.emerging: Occasionally uses attention strategy with adult prompting during tasks.
competency_level_descriptors.developing: Sometimes uses attention strategy independently during tasks.
competency_level_descriptors.competent: Consistently applies practised attention strategy independently throughout tasks.
competency_level_descriptors.extending: Consistently applies strategy and can teach it to others.

criteria.decomposition_rationale: Two criteria are needed because using a strategy and reflecting on focus factors represent distinct cognitive operations that can be independently demonstrated.

criteria.criterion_statement: I can describe what made it easier or harder to focus during a task.

competency_level_descriptors.no_evidence: Cannot identify focus factors after tasks.
competency_level_descriptors.emerging: Identifies simple focus factors with adult guidance and questioning.
competency_level_descriptors.developing: Sometimes identifies focus factors independently with basic explanations.
competency_level_descriptors.competent: Consistently describes specific factors that helped or hindered focus during tasks.
competency_level_descriptors.extending: Describes focus factors and connects them to strategy effectiveness.

criteria.decomposition_rationale: Two criteria are needed because using a strategy and reflecting on focus factors represent distinct cognitive operations that can be independently demonstrated.

criteria.criterion_statement: I can evaluate how an attention strategy affected my focus during a specific task.

competency_level_descriptors.no_evidence: No evaluation of strategy impact observed
competency_level_descriptors.emerging: Evaluates strategy impact with prompting and support
competency_level_descriptors.developing: Sometimes evaluates strategy impact independently
competency_level_descriptors.competent: Consistently evaluates how attention strategies affected focus during tasks
competency_level_descriptors.extending: Evaluates strategy impact and guides others in self-reflection

criteria.decomposition_rationale: The band statement contains two distinct cognitive operations: evaluating strategy effectiveness and explaining reasoning, which require separate assessment.

criteria.criterion_statement: I can explain why an attention strategy did or didn't help my focus.

competency_level_descriptors.no_evidence: No reasoning explanations provided
competency_level_descriptors.emerging: Explains strategy reasoning with prompting and guidance
competency_level_descriptors.developing: Sometimes explains why strategies worked or failed
competency_level_descriptors.competent: Consistently explains reasoning behind strategy effectiveness clearly
competency_level_descriptors.extending: Explains reasoning and helps others understand strategy mechanisms

criteria.decomposition_rationale: The band statement contains two distinct cognitive operations: evaluating strategy effectiveness and explaining reasoning, which require separate assessment.

criteria.criterion_statement: I can sustain attention during a challenging task for an extended period.

competency_level_descriptors.no_evidence: Attention frequently breaks during challenging tasks.
competency_level_descriptors.emerging: Sustains attention briefly during challenging tasks with prompting.
competency_level_descriptors.developing: Sustains attention for moderate periods during challenging tasks.
competency_level_descriptors.competent: Consistently sustains attention throughout challenging tasks without external reminders.
competency_level_descriptors.extending: Sustains deep focus and can describe attention management techniques.

criteria.decomposition_rationale: Sustaining attention is a distinct cognitive operation from identifying distractors or applying strategies.

criteria.criterion_statement: I can identify what is pulling my attention away from a task.

competency_level_descriptors.no_evidence: Cannot identify what causes attention to wander.
competency_level_descriptors.emerging: Occasionally notices attention has wandered with prompting.
competency_level_descriptors.developing: Sometimes identifies distractors after attention has wandered.
competency_level_descriptors.competent: Consistently recognises and can name specific distractors affecting focus.
competency_level_descriptors.extending: Anticipates potential distractors and explains their impact.

criteria.decomposition_rationale: Identifying distractors requires metacognitive awareness distinct from the other operations.

criteria.criterion_statement: I can use a deliberate strategy to return my attention to a task.

competency_level_descriptors.no_evidence: Does not use strategies to refocus attention.
competency_level_descriptors.emerging: Attempts to use attention strategies with prompting.
competency_level_descriptors.developing: Sometimes applies strategies to return focus independently.
competency_level_descriptors.competent: Consistently selects and applies effective strategies to refocus attention.
competency_level_descriptors.extending: Flexibly adapts strategies and explains their effectiveness.

criteria.decomposition_rationale: Strategic application of attention-return techniques is a separate cognitive operation requiring deliberate selection and execution of specific methods.

criteria.criterion_statement: I can retell a short experience with key details in sequence.

competency_level_descriptors.no_evidence: Cannot retell experience coherently
competency_level_descriptors.emerging: Retells experience with prompting, some details missing
competency_level_descriptors.developing: Retells experience with most key details present
competency_level_descriptors.competent: Clearly retells experience with key details in logical sequence
competency_level_descriptors.extending: Retells experience vividly and helps others understand the sequence

criteria.decomposition_rationale: Retelling an experience and identifying learning from it are distinct cognitive operations requiring separate assessment.

criteria.criterion_statement: I can identify and state one specific thing I learned from an experience.

competency_level_descriptors.no_evidence: Cannot identify learning from experience
competency_level_descriptors.emerging: States learning with prompting, may be vague
competency_level_descriptors.developing: Sometimes identifies specific learning without prompting
competency_level_descriptors.competent: Consistently states specific learning gained from the experience
competency_level_descriptors.extending: States learning clearly and explains how it applies elsewhere

criteria.decomposition_rationale: Retelling an experience and identifying learning from it are distinct cognitive operations requiring separate assessment.

criteria.criterion_statement: I can pause before making a choice to consider potential outcomes.

competency_level_descriptors.no_evidence: Does not pause before making choices.
competency_level_descriptors.emerging: Occasionally pauses before choosing when prompted by others.
competency_level_descriptors.developing: Sometimes pauses before choosing without being reminded.
competency_level_descriptors.competent: Consistently pauses to think before making choices without prompting.
competency_level_descriptors.extending: Consistently pauses and can explain why thinking first helps.

criteria.decomposition_rationale: This criterion captures the metacognitive 'stopping' behaviour that precedes the outcome analysis, which is a distinct observable action from identifying consequences.

criteria.criterion_statement: I can identify at least one helpful and one harmful possible outcome for a choice.

competency_level_descriptors.no_evidence: Cannot identify potential outcomes of choices.
competency_level_descriptors.emerging: Occasionally identifies one type of outcome when prompted.
competency_level_descriptors.developing: Sometimes identifies both helpful and harmful outcomes with support.
competency_level_descriptors.competent: Consistently identifies at least one helpful and one harmful outcome independently.
competency_level_descriptors.extending: Consistently identifies multiple outcomes and explains their reasoning clearly.

criteria.decomposition_rationale: This criterion focuses on the cognitive skill of consequence prediction, which is a separate demonstrable ability from the metacognitive pause behaviour.

criteria.criterion_statement: I can compare multiple options by identifying key differences between them.

competency_level_descriptors.no_evidence: No comparison of options attempted.
competency_level_descriptors.emerging: Identifies some differences between options with prompting.
competency_level_descriptors.developing: Identifies key differences between most options independently.
competency_level_descriptors.competent: Systematically compares multiple options by identifying relevant differences.
competency_level_descriptors.extending: Compares options thoroughly and explains comparison process.

criteria.decomposition_rationale: Three distinct cognitive operations require separate assessment: comparing options involves analysis, stating choice requires decision-making, and giving value-based reasons involves justification.

criteria.criterion_statement: I can clearly state my choice from available options.

competency_level_descriptors.no_evidence: No clear choice stated.
competency_level_descriptors.emerging: States a choice with prompting or clarification.
competency_level_descriptors.developing: States choice clearly most of the time.
competency_level_descriptors.competent: Consistently states clear, definitive choice from available options.
competency_level_descriptors.extending: States choice decisively and explains decision-making process.

criteria.decomposition_rationale: Three distinct cognitive operations require separate assessment: comparing options involves analysis, stating choice requires decision-making, and giving value-based reasons involves justification.

criteria.criterion_statement: I can give a reason for my choice that connects to my personal values.

competency_level_descriptors.no_evidence: No reasoning provided for choice.
competency_level_descriptors.emerging: Provides basic reasoning with prompting about values.
competency_level_descriptors.developing: Sometimes connects reasoning to personal values independently.
competency_level_descriptors.competent: Consistently provides clear reasoning that explicitly connects to personal values.
competency_level_descriptors.extending: Articulates value-based reasoning and explains value priorities.

criteria.decomposition_rationale: Three distinct cognitive operations require separate assessment: comparing options involves analysis, stating choice requires decision-making, and giving value-based reasons involves justification.

criteria.criterion_statement: I can explain the trade-offs involved in a decision by identifying what is gained and lost with different options.

competency_level_descriptors.no_evidence: Does not identify gains or losses in options
competency_level_descriptors.emerging: Identifies some gains or losses with prompting
competency_level_descriptors.developing: Identifies gains and losses for some options independently
competency_level_descriptors.competent: Clearly explains what is gained and lost across multiple decision options
competency_level_descriptors.extending: Articulates complex trade-offs and their interconnections

criteria.decomposition_rationale: Two distinct cognitive operations: explaining trade-offs requires analytical thinking about gains/losses, while justifying choices based on effects on people requires evaluative reasoning with empathy.

criteria.criterion_statement: I can justify my choice by considering how the decision affects different people involved.

competency_level_descriptors.no_evidence: Does not consider effects on others
competency_level_descriptors.emerging: Considers effects on one person with prompting
competency_level_descriptors.developing: Considers effects on some people without prompting
competency_level_descriptors.competent: Justifies choice by thoughtfully considering impacts on different people involved
competency_level_descriptors.extending: Justifies choice considering diverse perspectives and stakeholder needs

criteria.decomposition_rationale: Two distinct cognitive operations: explaining trade-offs requires analytical thinking about gains/losses, while justifying choices based on effects on people requires evaluative reasoning with empathy.

criteria.criterion_statement: I can name my body's basic needs such as food, water, sleep, and movement.

competency_level_descriptors.no_evidence: Cannot name basic body needs
competency_level_descriptors.emerging: Names 1-2 basic needs with prompting
competency_level_descriptors.developing: Names 2-3 basic needs independently
competency_level_descriptors.competent: Names 4+ basic needs (food, water, sleep, movement) clearly
competency_level_descriptors.extending: Names basic needs and explains why bodies need them

criteria.decomposition_rationale: Naming basic needs is a distinct cognitive act from practicing hygiene routines, requiring factual knowledge rather than procedural demonstration.

criteria.criterion_statement: I can practise simple personal hygiene routines with reminders.

competency_level_descriptors.no_evidence: Does not engage in hygiene routines
competency_level_descriptors.emerging: Participates in hygiene routines with significant guidance
competency_level_descriptors.developing: Follows hygiene routines with occasional reminders
competency_level_descriptors.competent: Consistently follows simple hygiene routines when reminded
competency_level_descriptors.extending: Follows hygiene routines and reminds others or self

criteria.decomposition_rationale: Practicing hygiene routines involves different procedural skills from naming body needs, requiring physical demonstration rather than verbal knowledge.

criteria.criterion_statement: I can explain how sleep, food, and movement affect how I feel.

competency_level_descriptors.no_evidence: Cannot connect health habits to feelings.
competency_level_descriptors.emerging: Names some connections with prompting or examples.
competency_level_descriptors.developing: Explains basic connections between habits and feelings sometimes.
competency_level_descriptors.competent: Clearly explains how sleep, food, and movement affect personal feelings and energy.
competency_level_descriptors.extending: Explains connections and helps others understand habit-feeling relationships.

criteria.decomposition_rationale: Two distinct cognitive operations: explaining causal relationships versus goal-setting behavior require separate assessment.

criteria.criterion_statement: I can set a goal for one healthy habit.

competency_level_descriptors.no_evidence: Does not identify or set health goals.
competency_level_descriptors.emerging: Identifies a habit to work on with guidance.
competency_level_descriptors.developing: Sets a basic goal for healthy habit change.
competency_level_descriptors.competent: Sets a clear, specific goal for improving one healthy habit.
competency_level_descriptors.extending: Sets specific goals and explains why the habit matters to them.

criteria.decomposition_rationale: Two distinct cognitive operations: explaining causal relationships versus goal-setting behavior require separate assessment.

criteria.criterion_statement: I can track one habit consistently for a week using a recording method.

competency_level_descriptors.no_evidence: No habit tracking attempted or recorded.
competency_level_descriptors.emerging: Records habit data for 2-3 days with prompting.
competency_level_descriptors.developing: Records habit data for most days independently.
competency_level_descriptors.competent: Consistently records habit data for all seven days using chosen method.
competency_level_descriptors.extending: Tracks habit comprehensively and suggests improvements to tracking method.

criteria.decomposition_rationale: Tracking represents a distinct data collection skill that can be assessed independently through record-keeping evidence.

criteria.criterion_statement: I can identify a specific barrier that affects my habit practice.

competency_level_descriptors.no_evidence: No barriers identified or mentioned.
competency_level_descriptors.emerging: Names a vague or general obstacle with prompting.
competency_level_descriptors.developing: Identifies a relevant barrier but may lack specificity.
competency_level_descriptors.competent: Clearly identifies a specific barrier that genuinely affects habit practice.
competency_level_descriptors.extending: Identifies barrier and recognizes its underlying causes or patterns.

criteria.decomposition_rationale: Barrier identification requires analytical thinking that is separate from tracking or explanation skills.

criteria.criterion_statement: I can explain my reasoning for wanting to keep or change a tracked habit.

competency_level_descriptors.no_evidence: No explanation of habit decisions provided.
competency_level_descriptors.emerging: States preference to keep or change with prompting.
competency_level_descriptors.developing: Gives basic reason for keeping or changing habit.
competency_level_descriptors.competent: Clearly explains reasoning with connection to personal goals or wellbeing.
competency_level_descriptors.extending: Explains reasoning and considers multiple factors or potential consequences.

criteria.decomposition_rationale: Explanation requires reflective reasoning skills distinct from data collection and barrier analysis, representing a separate cognitive operation.

criteria.criterion_statement: I can compare two sources of health information for credibility and usefulness.

competency_level_descriptors.no_evidence: Does not evaluate health information sources.
competency_level_descriptors.emerging: Identifies basic differences between sources with prompting.
competency_level_descriptors.developing: Compares sources using some evaluation criteria independently.
competency_level_descriptors.competent: Systematically compares sources for credibility, accuracy, and relevance to personal needs.
competency_level_descriptors.extending: Explains evaluation criteria and teaches others to assess sources critically.

criteria.decomposition_rationale: Comparing sources requires distinct evaluation skills separate from planning and adjusting habits.

criteria.criterion_statement: I can create a balanced weekly habits plan incorporating multiple health areas.

competency_level_descriptors.no_evidence: Does not create structured habit plans.
competency_level_descriptors.emerging: Creates simple plans with prompting for balance.
competency_level_descriptors.developing: Creates weekly plans addressing some health areas.
competency_level_descriptors.competent: Creates comprehensive weekly plans balancing sleep, nutrition, exercise, and wellbeing goals.
competency_level_descriptors.extending: Creates detailed plans and explains rationale for balance choices.

criteria.decomposition_rationale: Creating balanced plans requires synthesis skills distinct from information evaluation and plan adjustment.

criteria.criterion_statement: I can adjust my habits plan based on logged results and reflection.

competency_level_descriptors.no_evidence: Does not track or adjust plans.
competency_level_descriptors.emerging: Makes basic changes with guidance after logging.
competency_level_descriptors.developing: Adjusts some aspects based on logged data.
competency_level_descriptors.competent: Systematically reviews logged results and makes informed adjustments to improve outcomes.
competency_level_descriptors.extending: Explains adjustment reasoning and models reflective planning for others.

criteria.decomposition_rationale: Plan adjustment requires data analysis and reflection skills that are cognitively distinct from creating initial plans.

criteria.criterion_statement: The learner can identify trusted adults available for help and demonstrate simple self-care practices.

competency_level_descriptors.no_evidence: Cannot name trusted adults or self-care practices.
competency_level_descriptors.emerging: Names one trusted adult with prompting; attempts self-care with guidance.
competency_level_descriptors.developing: Sometimes identifies trusted adults and practices self-care without reminders.
competency_level_descriptors.competent: Consistently names trusted adults for help and demonstrates simple self-care routines independently.
competency_level_descriptors.extending: Consistently demonstrates self-care and explains why trusted adults are important.

criteria.decomposition_rationale: This is a single dispositional criterion combining help-seeking and self-care behaviours, as both reflect the same underlying wellbeing orientation observable at this developmental level.

criteria.criterion_statement: I can identify my body's early warning signs and choose a helpful self-care response.

competency_level_descriptors.no_evidence: Does not notice body signals or self-care needs.
competency_level_descriptors.emerging: Sometimes notices body signals with prompting and tries self-care strategies.
competency_level_descriptors.developing: Often recognises body's warning signs and selects appropriate self-care responses.
competency_level_descriptors.competent: Consistently identifies body's early warning signs and independently chooses effective self-care responses that help restore wellbeing.
competency_level_descriptors.extending: Consistently demonstrates body awareness and self-care, and can explain strategies to others.

criteria.decomposition_rationale: This is a single dispositional behaviour combining body awareness with self-care response selection, observable as one integrated capability.

criteria.criterion_statement: I can create a comprehensive self-care plan that includes strategies for both home and school environments and identify trusted support networks.

competency_level_descriptors.no_evidence: No self-care planning or support identification observed.
competency_level_descriptors.emerging: Creates basic self-care ideas with prompting; names few support people.
competency_level_descriptors.developing: Creates simple self-care plan for one environment; identifies some trusted people.
competency_level_descriptors.competent: Creates detailed self-care plan covering home and school strategies; identifies comprehensive support network.
competency_level_descriptors.extending: Creates sophisticated self-care plan and explains support network choices to others.

criteria.decomposition_rationale: This is a single dispositional criterion as it represents one integrated behaviour of planning and help-seeking that demonstrates the learner's developing self-care capacity across contexts.

criteria.criterion_statement: The learner maintains their key self-care routines during stressful times and describes one way they support a peer to do the same.

competency_level_descriptors.no_evidence: No self-care maintenance or peer support observed.
competency_level_descriptors.emerging: Occasionally maintains self-care routines when prompted during stress.
competency_level_descriptors.developing: Sometimes maintains self-care routines during stress independently.
competency_level_descriptors.competent: Consistently maintains self-care routines during stressful times and describes peer support strategies.
competency_level_descriptors.extending: Maintains self-care consistently and articulates how peer support strategies work.

criteria.decomposition_rationale: This is a single dispositional criterion as it represents one integrated behaviour pattern of resilient self-care that includes both personal maintenance and peer support elements observable together.

criteria.criterion_statement: I can correctly name private body parts using appropriate terminology.

competency_level_descriptors.no_evidence: Cannot name private body parts.
competency_level_descriptors.emerging: Names some private body parts with prompting or support.
competency_level_descriptors.developing: Names most private body parts correctly when asked.
competency_level_descriptors.competent: Confidently names all private body parts using correct terminology.
competency_level_descriptors.extending: Names private body parts correctly and explains why proper names are important.

criteria.decomposition_rationale: Two distinct criteria are needed as naming body parts requires factual knowledge while identifying trusted adults involves relationship awareness and safety understanding.

criteria.criterion_statement: I can identify my trusted adults who help keep me safe.

competency_level_descriptors.no_evidence: Cannot identify trusted adults.
competency_level_descriptors.emerging: Names one trusted adult with prompting or support.
competency_level_descriptors.developing: Identifies some trusted adults when asked about safety.
competency_level_descriptors.competent: Clearly names several trusted adults who help keep them safe.
competency_level_descriptors.extending: Names trusted adults and explains how they help with safety.

criteria.decomposition_rationale: Two distinct criteria are needed as naming body parts requires factual knowledge while identifying trusted adults involves relationship awareness and safety understanding.

criteria.criterion_statement: I can set personal boundaries and respect the boundaries of others.

competency_level_descriptors.no_evidence: Does not demonstrate boundary-setting or respecting others' boundaries.
competency_level_descriptors.emerging: Shows awareness of boundaries with adult prompting and support.
competency_level_descriptors.developing: Sometimes sets own boundaries and respects others' with occasional reminders.
competency_level_descriptors.competent: Consistently sets personal boundaries and respects others' boundaries without prompting.
competency_level_descriptors.extending: Models boundary-setting for peers and explains why boundaries are important.

criteria.decomposition_rationale: Setting and respecting boundaries involves distinct but related skills that can be assessed together as they represent the same cognitive operation applied to boundary management.

criteria.criterion_statement: I can ask for clear consent before actions that affect others.

competency_level_descriptors.no_evidence: Does not ask for consent before actions affecting others.
competency_level_descriptors.emerging: Sometimes asks for consent when prompted by adults.
competency_level_descriptors.developing: Often asks for consent but may need reminders in some situations.
competency_level_descriptors.competent: Consistently asks for clear consent before actions that affect others.
competency_level_descriptors.extending: Models consent-seeking for peers and explains why consent matters.

criteria.decomposition_rationale: Asking for consent represents a distinct cognitive operation from boundary management, requiring different communication and decision-making skills.

criteria.criterion_statement: I can seek help from trusted adults when I feel pressured or unsafe.

competency_level_descriptors.no_evidence: Does not seek help when feeling pressured.
competency_level_descriptors.emerging: Recognises pressure but needs prompting to seek help.
competency_level_descriptors.developing: Sometimes seeks help when pressured but may hesitate.
competency_level_descriptors.competent: Consistently seeks help from trusted adults when feeling pressured or unsafe.
competency_level_descriptors.extending: Helps peers recognise when to seek help and models help-seeking behaviour.

criteria.decomposition_rationale: Help-seeking when pressured involves different skills than boundary-setting or consent-asking, including recognising pressure situations and knowing how to access support.

criteria.criterion_statement: I can apply consent skills in non-sexual and online contexts.

competency_level_descriptors.no_evidence: No demonstration of consent skills in contexts.
competency_level_descriptors.emerging: Applies basic consent skills with prompting in simple scenarios.
competency_level_descriptors.developing: Applies consent skills in some non-sexual and online contexts.
competency_level_descriptors.competent: Consistently applies consent skills across various non-sexual and online situations appropriately.
competency_level_descriptors.extending: Applies consent skills fluently and can explain their importance to others.

criteria.decomposition_rationale: This represents a distinct cognitive operation of applying procedural knowledge in specific contexts.

criteria.criterion_statement: I can state my boundaries clearly to peers.

competency_level_descriptors.no_evidence: No clear boundary communication observed with peers.
competency_level_descriptors.emerging: States some boundaries with prompting or unclear communication.
competency_level_descriptors.developing: States boundaries to peers with increasing clarity and confidence.
competency_level_descriptors.competent: Consistently states personal boundaries clearly and directly to peers when needed.
competency_level_descriptors.extending: States boundaries clearly and helps peers understand appropriate boundary communication.

criteria.decomposition_rationale: Boundary communication is a separate cognitive operation from applying consent skills, requiring different communication competencies.

criteria.criterion_statement: I can recognise situations where power imbalances or peer pressure create risks to consent and safety.

competency_level_descriptors.no_evidence: Cannot identify risky power or pressure situations.
competency_level_descriptors.emerging: Identifies obvious risk situations with prompting and guidance.
competency_level_descriptors.developing: Identifies some risk situations independently but misses subtle dynamics.
competency_level_descriptors.competent: Consistently identifies various power imbalances and peer pressure situations that compromise safety.
competency_level_descriptors.extending: Identifies risks and explains underlying dynamics to others.

criteria.decomposition_rationale: Recognition of risks is a distinct cognitive skill from planning responses or supporting others.

criteria.criterion_statement: I can clearly state my refusal and articulate a specific exit plan when facing pressure situations.

competency_level_descriptors.no_evidence: Cannot articulate refusal or exit strategies.
competency_level_descriptors.emerging: States basic refusal with prompting but exit plan unclear.
competency_level_descriptors.developing: States refusal clearly but exit plan lacks specific details.
competency_level_descriptors.competent: Clearly states refusal and describes specific, practical exit strategies for pressure situations.
competency_level_descriptors.extending: Models assertive refusal and detailed exit planning for peers.

criteria.decomposition_rationale: Active response skills (refusing and exiting) require different competencies than recognition or peer support.

criteria.criterion_statement: I can describe how I would help a peer access appropriate support when they face safety concerns.

competency_level_descriptors.no_evidence: Cannot describe peer support strategies.
competency_level_descriptors.emerging: Describes basic peer support with prompting and guidance.
competency_level_descriptors.developing: Describes some appropriate support strategies but misses key elements.
competency_level_descriptors.competent: Describes clear, appropriate strategies for supporting peers to access help for safety concerns.
competency_level_descriptors.extending: Describes comprehensive peer support and demonstrates bystander intervention skills.

criteria.decomposition_rationale: Supporting others requires distinct interpersonal and help-seeking knowledge beyond personal safety skills.

criteria.criterion_statement: I can explain the basic physical and emotional changes that happen during puberty.

competency_level_descriptors.no_evidence: Cannot describe any puberty changes
competency_level_descriptors.emerging: Names one or two puberty changes with prompting
competency_level_descriptors.developing: Describes several physical changes but needs support for emotional aspects
competency_level_descriptors.competent: Clearly explains both physical and emotional changes that occur during puberty
competency_level_descriptors.extending: Explains puberty changes and helps others understand them

criteria.decomposition_rationale: This targets the specific cognitive act of explaining puberty changes, which is distinct from the procedural skills that follow.

criteria.criterion_statement: I can use appropriate personal hygiene routines consistently.

competency_level_descriptors.no_evidence: Does not follow hygiene routines
competency_level_descriptors.emerging: Follows basic hygiene with frequent reminders and support
competency_level_descriptors.developing: Follows hygiene routines with occasional reminders about specific practices
competency_level_descriptors.competent: Consistently maintains appropriate personal hygiene routines without prompting
competency_level_descriptors.extending: Models good hygiene and explains importance to others

criteria.decomposition_rationale: This represents a procedural skill that is cognitively distinct from explaining concepts or finding information.

criteria.criterion_statement: I can find reliable health information from trusted sources.

competency_level_descriptors.no_evidence: Cannot identify reliable health sources
competency_level_descriptors.emerging: Identifies one trusted source with guidance about reliability
competency_level_descriptors.developing: Finds health information but needs support distinguishing reliable sources
competency_level_descriptors.competent: Successfully locates reliable health information from multiple trusted sources
competency_level_descriptors.extending: Evaluates source reliability and helps others find trustworthy information

criteria.decomposition_rationale: Information literacy represents a distinct cognitive operation from explaining concepts or applying procedures, requiring evaluation and selection skills.

criteria.criterion_statement: I can manage my personal hygiene practices specifically related to puberty changes.

competency_level_descriptors.no_evidence: No hygiene practices demonstrated.
competency_level_descriptors.emerging: Basic hygiene practices with frequent prompting and guidance.
competency_level_descriptors.developing: Some independent hygiene practices with occasional reminders needed.
competency_level_descriptors.competent: Consistently manages personal hygiene independently with understanding of puberty-related needs.
competency_level_descriptors.extending: Models good hygiene practices and explains rationale to others.

criteria.decomposition_rationale: This criterion focuses on the practical application and management of hygiene routines, which is distinct from the cognitive tasks of identifying sources and explaining reproduction.

criteria.criterion_statement: I can identify and distinguish between trusted and unreliable sources of health information.

competency_level_descriptors.no_evidence: Cannot identify reliable health sources.
competency_level_descriptors.emerging: Identifies some trusted sources with significant guidance and prompting.
competency_level_descriptors.developing: Sometimes distinguishes between reliable and unreliable health sources independently.
competency_level_descriptors.competent: Consistently identifies trusted health information sources and explains why they are reliable.
competency_level_descriptors.extending: Evaluates source credibility and teaches others evaluation strategies.

criteria.decomposition_rationale: Source identification requires distinct evaluative skills separate from hygiene management and reproductive knowledge.

criteria.criterion_statement: I can explain the basic biological processes and concepts of human reproduction.

competency_level_descriptors.no_evidence: Cannot explain reproduction concepts.
competency_level_descriptors.emerging: Explains basic reproduction concepts with prompting and support.
competency_level_descriptors.developing: Sometimes explains reproduction basics accurately with minor gaps.
competency_level_descriptors.competent: Clearly explains fundamental human reproduction processes using appropriate terminology.
competency_level_descriptors.extending: Explains reproduction comprehensively and connects to broader health concepts.

criteria.decomposition_rationale: Explaining reproduction involves different cognitive demands (factual recall and conceptual understanding) than the practical and evaluative skills in the other criteria.

criteria.criterion_statement: I can assess risks related to STIs, pregnancy, and substance use.

competency_level_descriptors.no_evidence: Cannot identify or evaluate health-related risks.
competency_level_descriptors.emerging: Identifies basic risks with prompting but assessment lacks detail.
competency_level_descriptors.developing: Assesses some risks accurately but may miss important factors.
competency_level_descriptors.competent: Accurately assesses multiple risks and considers relevant factors affecting severity.
competency_level_descriptors.extending: Comprehensively assesses risks and explains reasoning to others.

criteria.decomposition_rationale: Risk assessment is a distinct cognitive operation requiring evaluation and analysis skills separate from explaining protection methods.

criteria.criterion_statement: I can explain protection methods for sexual health and substance use.

competency_level_descriptors.no_evidence: Cannot explain any protection methods.
competency_level_descriptors.emerging: Explains basic protection methods with prompting and guidance.
competency_level_descriptors.developing: Explains some protection methods but may lack detail or accuracy.
competency_level_descriptors.competent: Clearly explains multiple protection methods and their effectiveness.
competency_level_descriptors.extending: Explains protection methods comprehensively and addresses misconceptions.

criteria.decomposition_rationale: Explaining protection methods requires different cognitive skills from risk assessment and involves declarative knowledge application.

criteria.criterion_statement: I can identify steps to access confidential health advice.

competency_level_descriptors.no_evidence: Cannot identify ways to access health advice.
competency_level_descriptors.emerging: Identifies basic steps to access health advice with prompting.
competency_level_descriptors.developing: Identifies some steps but may miss confidentiality aspects.
competency_level_descriptors.competent: Clearly identifies multiple pathways to confidential health advice.
competency_level_descriptors.extending: Identifies comprehensive pathways and explains confidentiality importance.

criteria.decomposition_rationale: Identifying access steps involves procedural knowledge distinct from risk assessment and protection explanation, requiring knowledge of health systems and services.

criteria.criterion_statement: I can take turns in conversation by waiting for others to finish speaking before I respond.

competency_level_descriptors.no_evidence: Does not wait for others to finish speaking.
competency_level_descriptors.emerging: Sometimes waits for others to finish with adult prompting.
competency_level_descriptors.developing: Usually waits for others to finish speaking without prompting.
competency_level_descriptors.competent: Consistently waits for others to finish speaking and responds appropriately in conversations.
competency_level_descriptors.extending: Models turn-taking and helps others practice waiting their turn.

criteria.decomposition_rationale: Taking turns, using kind words, and making I-statements are three distinct interpersonal communication skills that can be independently assessed and developed.

criteria.criterion_statement: I can use kind words when speaking with others.

competency_level_descriptors.no_evidence: Uses unkind or hurtful words regularly.
competency_level_descriptors.emerging: Uses kind words occasionally when reminded by adults.
competency_level_descriptors.developing: Uses kind words most of the time without reminders.
competency_level_descriptors.competent: Consistently chooses kind words when speaking with peers and adults.
competency_level_descriptors.extending: Models kind language and helps others choose respectful words.

criteria.decomposition_rationale: Taking turns, using kind words, and making I-statements are three distinct interpersonal communication skills that can be independently assessed and developed.

criteria.criterion_statement: I can use an I-statement to express my feelings or needs.

competency_level_descriptors.no_evidence: Does not use I-statements when expressing feelings.
competency_level_descriptors.emerging: Uses simple I-statements with adult prompting and support.
competency_level_descriptors.developing: Uses I-statements sometimes when expressing feelings or needs.
competency_level_descriptors.competent: Uses I-statements regularly to express feelings and needs clearly to others.
competency_level_descriptors.extending: Models I-statement use and teaches others this communication skill.

criteria.decomposition_rationale: Taking turns, using kind words, and making I-statements are three distinct interpersonal communication skills that can be independently assessed and developed.

criteria.criterion_statement: The learner can use I-statements to clearly express their personal needs.

competency_level_descriptors.no_evidence: Does not use I-statements when expressing needs.
competency_level_descriptors.emerging: Uses I-statements occasionally with adult prompting to express needs.
competency_level_descriptors.developing: Uses I-statements sometimes without prompting when expressing needs.
competency_level_descriptors.competent: Consistently uses I-statements to clearly communicate personal needs in various situations.
competency_level_descriptors.extending: Consistently uses I-statements and can explain why this communication approach is effective.

criteria.decomposition_rationale: This criterion focuses on the specific skill of using I-statements for need expression, which is a foundational communication technique at this developmental level.

criteria.criterion_statement: The learner can ask questions to understand others' boundaries.

competency_level_descriptors.no_evidence: Does not ask about others' boundaries.
competency_level_descriptors.emerging: Asks about boundaries occasionally with adult prompting or reminders.
competency_level_descriptors.developing: Sometimes asks about boundaries without prompting in social situations.
competency_level_descriptors.competent: Consistently asks appropriate questions to understand and respect others' boundaries in interactions.
competency_level_descriptors.extending: Consistently inquires about boundaries and can model this behavior for peers.

criteria.decomposition_rationale: Boundary awareness represents a distinct social skill that requires separate assessment from self-expression and problem-solving.

criteria.criterion_statement: The learner can propose two different options when seeking fair solutions to problems.

competency_level_descriptors.no_evidence: Does not offer multiple solution options.
competency_level_descriptors.emerging: Proposes two options occasionally with adult guidance and prompting.
competency_level_descriptors.developing: Sometimes proposes two options without prompting when solving problems.
competency_level_descriptors.competent: Consistently generates and presents two different fair options when working toward solutions.
competency_level_descriptors.extending: Consistently proposes multiple options and can explain why offering choices promotes fairness.

criteria.decomposition_rationale: Option generation for fair problem-solving is a distinct cognitive skill that involves perspective-taking and creative thinking, separate from the communication skills above.

criteria.criterion_statement: I can listen in a disagreement without interrupting others while they speak.

competency_level_descriptors.no_evidence: Interrupts frequently during disagreements
competency_level_descriptors.emerging: Occasionally listens without interrupting when reminded
competency_level_descriptors.developing: Sometimes listens without interrupting in disagreements
competency_level_descriptors.competent: Consistently listens without interrupting during disagreements
competency_level_descriptors.extending: Models active listening and explains its importance

criteria.decomposition_rationale: Three distinct cognitive operations are involved: listening skills, respectful word choice, and repair routines each require separate assessment to determine specific areas of strength or need.

criteria.criterion_statement: I can choose words that stay respectful when communicating during disagreements.

competency_level_descriptors.no_evidence: Uses disrespectful language during disagreements
competency_level_descriptors.emerging: Occasionally chooses respectful words with prompting
competency_level_descriptors.developing: Sometimes chooses respectful words independently
competency_level_descriptors.competent: Consistently chooses respectful words during disagreements
competency_level_descriptors.extending: Models respectful communication and coaches others

criteria.decomposition_rationale: Three distinct cognitive operations are involved: listening skills, respectful word choice, and repair routines each require separate assessment to determine specific areas of strength or need.

criteria.criterion_statement: I can use a simple repair routine when communication breaks down.

competency_level_descriptors.no_evidence: Does not attempt to repair communication
competency_level_descriptors.emerging: Attempts simple repair with significant guidance
competency_level_descriptors.developing: Uses simple repair routine with minimal prompting
competency_level_descriptors.competent: Consistently applies simple repair routine when needed
competency_level_descriptors.extending: Uses repair routine effectively and teaches others

criteria.decomposition_rationale: Three distinct cognitive operations are involved: listening skills, respectful word choice, and repair routines each require separate assessment to determine specific areas of strength or need.

criteria.criterion_statement: I can state my boundaries clearly in interpersonal interactions.

competency_level_descriptors.no_evidence: No clear boundary statements observed.
competency_level_descriptors.emerging: States boundaries with prompting or support from others.
competency_level_descriptors.developing: States boundaries clearly in some situations without prompting.
competency_level_descriptors.competent: Consistently states personal boundaries clearly and directly in various interpersonal situations.
competency_level_descriptors.extending: Models clear boundary communication and helps others articulate their boundaries.

criteria.decomposition_rationale: This targets the specific skill of boundary communication, which is a distinct cognitive operation from paraphrasing or word choice.

criteria.criterion_statement: I can paraphrase what others say before responding in conversations.

competency_level_descriptors.no_evidence: No paraphrasing before responding observed.
competency_level_descriptors.emerging: Occasionally paraphrases with reminders or in structured settings.
competency_level_descriptors.developing: Sometimes paraphrases before responding without prompting in conversations.
competency_level_descriptors.competent: Consistently paraphrases others' statements before responding to demonstrate understanding and ensure clarity.
competency_level_descriptors.extending: Uses sophisticated paraphrasing techniques and teaches this skill to others.

criteria.decomposition_rationale: Paraphrasing represents a distinct cognitive operation of processing and reflecting understanding separate from boundary-setting and word choice.

criteria.criterion_statement: I can choose appropriate words for different situations including during conflict.

competency_level_descriptors.no_evidence: Word choice not adapted to situations.
competency_level_descriptors.emerging: Shows awareness of appropriate words with guidance during conflicts.
competency_level_descriptors.developing: Sometimes chooses situation-appropriate words including in some conflict situations.
competency_level_descriptors.competent: Consistently selects words appropriate to context and audience, maintaining respect even during difficult conversations.
competency_level_descriptors.extending: Demonstrates sophisticated language adaptation and helps others communicate effectively in challenging situations.

criteria.decomposition_rationale: Word choice and tone adaptation is a separate communication skill requiring different cognitive processing than boundary-setting or paraphrasing.

criteria.criterion_statement: I can take responsibility for a classroom job and explain how my actions help the class community function well.

competency_level_descriptors.no_evidence: Does not engage with classroom jobs consistently.
competency_level_descriptors.emerging: Completes classroom job with reminders but cannot explain its purpose.
competency_level_descriptors.developing: Completes classroom job independently and gives simple explanations of helpfulness.
competency_level_descriptors.competent: Consistently completes classroom job and clearly explains how it helps the class community.
competency_level_descriptors.extending: Consistently completes classroom job and can teach others why these roles matter.

criteria.decomposition_rationale: This is a single dispositional criterion as it captures one observable behavior pattern of taking responsibility and connecting personal actions to community benefit.

criteria.criterion_statement: I can notice when peers are excluded and take action to include them in group activities.

competency_level_descriptors.no_evidence: Does not notice or respond to peer exclusion.
competency_level_descriptors.emerging: Sometimes notices exclusion but needs prompting to take inclusive action.
competency_level_descriptors.developing: Notices exclusion and occasionally invites others to join without prompting.
competency_level_descriptors.competent: Consistently notices when peers are left out and actively invites them to participate in activities.
competency_level_descriptors.extending: Proactively includes others and explains why inclusion matters to everyone.

criteria.decomposition_rationale: This is a single dispositional behaviour combining awareness of exclusion with inclusive action, observable as one unified response pattern.

criteria.criterion_statement: The learner can identify a school need, propose a simple project to address it, and complete the project collaboratively with teammates.

competency_level_descriptors.no_evidence: No engagement in identifying needs or proposing projects.
competency_level_descriptors.emerging: Identifies school needs with prompting and participates in proposed projects.
competency_level_descriptors.developing: Sometimes identifies needs independently and proposes basic project ideas with support.
competency_level_descriptors.competent: Consistently identifies school needs, proposes viable simple projects, and completes them through effective teamwork.
competency_level_descriptors.extending: Leads project identification and completion while articulating project impact to others.

criteria.decomposition_rationale: This is a single dispositional behaviour that integrates need identification, project proposal, and collaborative completion as one observable community engagement pattern at this developmental level.

criteria.criterion_statement: I can collaborate effectively with diverse peers on community projects, demonstrate measurable impact, and articulate personal connection to the work.

competency_level_descriptors.no_evidence: No community collaboration attempts observed.
competency_level_descriptors.emerging: Participates in group projects with prompting about diversity and impact.
competency_level_descriptors.developing: Works with diverse peers on projects, sometimes shows impact or explains personal connection.
competency_level_descriptors.competent: Consistently collaborates across differences, demonstrates project impact, and clearly explains why work matters personally.
competency_level_descriptors.extending: Models inclusive collaboration and helps others understand community impact and personal purpose.

criteria.decomposition_rationale: This dispositional learning target represents integrated community engagement behaviour that must be assessed as a unified demonstration of collaborative capability, impact awareness, and personal purpose.

criteria.criterion_statement: I can name body signals that tell me something about how I feel.

competency_level_descriptors.no_evidence: Cannot name body signals related to feelings.
competency_level_descriptors.emerging: Names one or two body signals with prompting.
competency_level_descriptors.developing: Names several body signals without prompting.
competency_level_descriptors.competent: Names multiple body signals and connects them to feelings.
competency_level_descriptors.extending: Names body signals and explains how they indicate feelings.

criteria.decomposition_rationale: This criterion focuses on the foundational knowledge of identifying physical sensations as indicators of emotional states, which is distinct from understanding the biological functions of sleep and food.

criteria.criterion_statement: I can describe what sleep and food do for my body and brain.

competency_level_descriptors.no_evidence: Cannot describe functions of sleep or food.
competency_level_descriptors.emerging: Describes basic functions of sleep or food with prompting.
competency_level_descriptors.developing: Describes some functions of sleep and food without prompting.
competency_level_descriptors.competent: Describes multiple functions of sleep and food for body and brain.
competency_level_descriptors.extending: Describes functions and explains why sleep and food are important.

criteria.decomposition_rationale: This criterion addresses understanding of biological functions, which requires different knowledge and cognitive processes than identifying body signals related to emotions.

criteria.criterion_statement: I can explain what happens in my body during a fight, flight, or freeze response.

competency_level_descriptors.no_evidence: Cannot describe stress response changes
competency_level_descriptors.emerging: Names one or two body changes during stress with prompting
competency_level_descriptors.developing: Describes some body changes but may confuse response types
competency_level_descriptors.competent: Clearly explains physical changes in fight, flight, or freeze responses with accurate examples
competency_level_descriptors.extending: Explains stress responses and can teach or demonstrate understanding to others

criteria.decomposition_rationale: This represents one distinct physiological concept that requires understanding the body's acute stress response mechanisms.

criteria.criterion_statement: I can describe the three parts of the habit loop.

competency_level_descriptors.no_evidence: Cannot identify habit loop parts
competency_level_descriptors.emerging: Names one or two habit loop components with prompting
competency_level_descriptors.developing: Identifies most habit loop parts but may miss connections
competency_level_descriptors.competent: Accurately describes all three parts of the habit loop and their relationships
competency_level_descriptors.extending: Describes habit loop components and applies framework to analyze specific behaviors

criteria.decomposition_rationale: This represents a separate behavioral mechanism concept distinct from stress physiology, requiring understanding of cue-routine-reward cycles.

criteria.criterion_statement: I can explain how the amygdala and prefrontal cortex interact during a stressful moment.

competency_level_descriptors.no_evidence: Cannot explain brain region interactions during stress.
competency_level_descriptors.emerging: Names amygdala and prefrontal cortex but unclear about their interaction.
competency_level_descriptors.developing: Explains basic roles but interaction description lacks detail or accuracy.
competency_level_descriptors.competent: Clearly explains how amygdala triggers stress response and prefrontal cortex regulates it during stressful moments.
competency_level_descriptors.extending: Explains interaction and can give specific examples of this process.

criteria.decomposition_rationale: This represents understanding a specific neurobiological mechanism requiring distinct knowledge of brain anatomy and stress physiology.

criteria.criterion_statement: I can explain what neuroplasticity means.

competency_level_descriptors.no_evidence: Cannot define or explain neuroplasticity.
competency_level_descriptors.emerging: Mentions brain change but explanation is vague or incorrect.
competency_level_descriptors.developing: Basic understanding that brain can change but lacks detail.
competency_level_descriptors.competent: Clearly explains neuroplasticity as the brain's ability to reorganize and form new connections.
competency_level_descriptors.extending: Explains concept and connects it to learning or habit formation.

criteria.decomposition_rationale: Neuroplasticity is a distinct foundational concept separate from stress mechanisms, requiring separate assessment of this core neuroscience principle.

criteria.criterion_statement: I can describe how the habit loop explains behaviour change at the level of mechanism.

competency_level_descriptors.no_evidence: Cannot connect habit loop to behaviour change.
competency_level_descriptors.emerging: Recalls habit loop parts but unclear about behaviour change mechanism.
competency_level_descriptors.developing: Describes habit loop and mentions behaviour change but lacks mechanistic detail.
competency_level_descriptors.competent: Clearly describes how cue-routine-reward cycle creates automatic behaviours and enables behaviour change through modification.
competency_level_descriptors.extending: Describes mechanism and can apply it to specific behaviour change scenarios.

criteria.decomposition_rationale: This requires distinct understanding of how habit formation works mechanistically, building on previous band knowledge but requiring deeper mechanistic explanation.

criteria.criterion_statement: I can use accurate neuroscience vocabulary to explain the interactions between stress, emotion, attention, and habit as an integrated system.

competency_level_descriptors.no_evidence: No neuroscience vocabulary used or system connections explained.
competency_level_descriptors.emerging: Uses some neuroscience terms with prompting to describe isolated components.
competency_level_descriptors.developing: Uses neuroscience vocabulary to explain some connections between stress, emotion, attention and habit.
competency_level_descriptors.competent: Accurately uses neuroscience vocabulary to explain how stress, emotion, attention and habit interact as an integrated system.
competency_level_descriptors.extending: Explains system interactions with precise vocabulary and can teach others these concepts.

criteria.decomposition_rationale: This represents a complex understanding (U-level) requiring integration of multiple neurobiological processes into a coherent systems explanation.

criteria.criterion_statement: I can describe the role of the HPA axis in the stress response mechanism.

competency_level_descriptors.no_evidence: Cannot identify or describe HPA axis components.
competency_level_descriptors.emerging: Names some HPA axis components with prompting but unclear on function.
competency_level_descriptors.developing: Describes basic HPA axis components and their general role in stress response.
competency_level_descriptors.competent: Accurately describes the HPA axis pathway and explains its specific role in coordinating stress responses.
competency_level_descriptors.extending: Describes HPA axis function and connects it to broader physiological regulation systems.

criteria.decomposition_rationale: This is a distinct factual knowledge (K-level) criterion about a specific neurobiological pathway that can be assessed independently from the systems understanding.

criteria.criterion_statement: I can identify whether a health claim comes from a trusted adult or source.

competency_level_descriptors.no_evidence: Cannot distinguish between trusted and untrusted sources.
competency_level_descriptors.emerging: Identifies trusted sources occasionally with adult prompting and support.
competency_level_descriptors.developing: Identifies trusted sources sometimes, showing inconsistent recognition patterns.
competency_level_descriptors.competent: Consistently identifies whether health claims come from trusted adults or reliable sources.
competency_level_descriptors.extending: Identifies trusted sources consistently and can teach others about source reliability.

criteria.decomposition_rationale: Two criteria are needed because identifying trusted sources and explaining reasoning are distinct cognitive operations at different levels - recognition versus articulation of thinking process.

criteria.criterion_statement: I can explain my reasoning for trusting or not trusting a health information source.

competency_level_descriptors.no_evidence: Cannot explain reasoning about source trustworthiness.
competency_level_descriptors.emerging: Explains reasoning occasionally with prompting, often using simple statements.
competency_level_descriptors.developing: Explains reasoning sometimes independently, using basic justifications for trust decisions.
competency_level_descriptors.competent: Consistently explains clear reasoning for why they trust or don't trust health information sources.
competency_level_descriptors.extending: Explains detailed reasoning and can help others understand source evaluation thinking.

criteria.decomposition_rationale: Two criteria are needed because identifying trusted sources and explaining reasoning are distinct cognitive operations at different levels - recognition versus articulation of thinking process.

criteria.criterion_statement: I can compare two health information sources by identifying key differences between them.

competency_level_descriptors.no_evidence: Cannot identify differences between health sources.
competency_level_descriptors.emerging: Identifies basic differences with prompting and support.
competency_level_descriptors.developing: Sometimes identifies key differences between sources independently.
competency_level_descriptors.competent: Consistently identifies and clearly describes key differences between health information sources.
competency_level_descriptors.extending: Systematically compares sources and explains comparison approach to others.

criteria.decomposition_rationale: This targets the comparison cognitive operation, which requires students to systematically examine and contrast two sources before making trust judgements.

criteria.criterion_statement: I can describe which health information source I trust more and explain my reasoning.

competency_level_descriptors.no_evidence: Cannot explain trust preferences for health sources.
competency_level_descriptors.emerging: States preference but reasoning unclear or prompted.
competency_level_descriptors.developing: Sometimes explains trust reasoning with basic justification.
competency_level_descriptors.competent: Clearly describes preferred source and provides logical reasoning for trust decision.
competency_level_descriptors.extending: Articulates nuanced reasoning and helps others evaluate source trustworthiness.

criteria.decomposition_rationale: This targets the evaluation and justification cognitive operation, which is distinct from comparison and requires students to make reasoned judgements about trustworthiness.

criteria.criterion_statement: I can examine and categorise the type of evidence supporting a health claim.

competency_level_descriptors.no_evidence: Cannot identify evidence types in health claims.
competency_level_descriptors.emerging: Identifies basic evidence types with prompting and support.
competency_level_descriptors.developing: Sometimes categorises evidence types but may miss key distinctions.
competency_level_descriptors.competent: Consistently examines and accurately categorises different types of evidence in health claims.
competency_level_descriptors.extending: Categorises evidence types and explains their relative strengths and limitations.

criteria.decomposition_rationale: This addresses the first cognitive operation of examining and analysing evidence types, which requires specific analytical skills distinct from bias detection.

criteria.criterion_statement: I can identify potential sources of bias in health information providers.

competency_level_descriptors.no_evidence: Cannot identify bias in health information sources.
competency_level_descriptors.emerging: Identifies obvious bias with prompting and guidance.
competency_level_descriptors.developing: Sometimes recognises potential bias but reasoning may be incomplete.
competency_level_descriptors.competent: Consistently identifies whether sources have reasons to be biased and explains those reasons.
competency_level_descriptors.extending: Identifies bias and explains how it might influence the information presented.

criteria.decomposition_rationale: This addresses the second cognitive operation of identifying bias, which involves different analytical skills from evidence evaluation and can be assessed independently.

criteria.criterion_statement: I can compare the quality of evidence supporting different sides of a contested health claim.

competency_level_descriptors.no_evidence: Does not attempt evidence comparison.
competency_level_descriptors.emerging: Compares evidence with significant prompting and support.
competency_level_descriptors.developing: Compares evidence quality with occasional guidance needed.
competency_level_descriptors.competent: Independently compares evidence quality across different sides of contested health claims.
competency_level_descriptors.extending: Compares evidence quality and explains comparison methodology.

criteria.decomposition_rationale: This criterion focuses specifically on the cognitive act of comparing evidence quality, which is distinct from identifying arguments or justifying conclusions.

criteria.criterion_statement: I can identify the strongest and weakest arguments in a contested health claim debate.

competency_level_descriptors.no_evidence: Does not identify argument strengths.
competency_level_descriptors.emerging: Identifies argument strength with substantial prompting and examples.
competency_level_descriptors.developing: Identifies strongest and weakest arguments with some guidance.
competency_level_descriptors.competent: Independently identifies strongest and weakest arguments in contested health debates.
competency_level_descriptors.extending: Identifies argument strength and explains evaluation criteria used.

criteria.decomposition_rationale: This criterion addresses the specific analytical skill of ranking argument strength, which requires different cognitive processes than evidence comparison.

criteria.criterion_statement: I can justify my conclusion about a contested health claim using evidence-based reasoning.

competency_level_descriptors.no_evidence: Provides no justification for conclusions.
competency_level_descriptors.emerging: Attempts justification with significant prompting and structure provided.
competency_level_descriptors.developing: Justifies conclusions using evidence with occasional support needed.
competency_level_descriptors.competent: Independently justifies conclusions about contested health claims using clear evidence-based reasoning.
competency_level_descriptors.extending: Justifies conclusions and acknowledges limitations or uncertainties.

criteria.decomposition_rationale: Justification represents a higher-order synthesis skill that builds on but is distinct from the analytical skills of comparing evidence and identifying argument strength.

criteria.criterion_statement: I can describe one thing I noticed about how I reacted to a situation.

competency_level_descriptors.no_evidence: Cannot describe own reaction to situation.
competency_level_descriptors.emerging: Describes own reaction with prompting and support.
competency_level_descriptors.developing: Sometimes describes own reaction without prompting.
competency_level_descriptors.competent: Consistently describes one specific thing noticed about own reaction to situation.
competency_level_descriptors.extending: Describes reaction and explains why noticing is important.

criteria.decomposition_rationale: This criterion focuses on the self-awareness component - noticing and describing one's own reaction to a situation.

criteria.criterion_statement: I can say what I might do differently next time.

competency_level_descriptors.no_evidence: Cannot suggest different response for next time.
competency_level_descriptors.emerging: Suggests different response with prompting and guidance.
competency_level_descriptors.developing: Sometimes suggests what to do differently without prompting.
competency_level_descriptors.competent: Consistently suggests specific different response for similar future situations.
